Auto industry mocks Tesla.

Got pushed out / sidelined / mocked. 
Ok, I’ll make my own AI lab.
California mocked and blocked him?  
Ok, I’ll move to Texas and make Star City.
Democrats mock him.
Ok, I’ll help Trump win.

Maybe the fastest way to change something on Earth is to find people in charge of it and encourage them to mock Elon?

kept.text:
@emmysotis Switching your PayPal account to a business account is a terrible idea unless you have very detailed documentation of what you're selling and you have receipts of cost of goods and sales. PayPal will shut your account down and take all the money out of your account and ban you for life. It's happened to many people I know and they tried to do it to me

Be very careful- I usually tell people just don't do this at all and if you can just don't use PayPal, even if you have a legit business. Also you're not going to stay anonymous on an invoice. If you did, when they demand you send them all this documentation (and they will), you won't have any way to prove it's even you.

kept.text:
DO NOT LIE TO PayPal or misrepresent a business identity!

I do want to be clear. I do not think OP meant harm. I think they were trying to share something they believed would help people protect their privacy. A PayPal Business account is not a fake name shield. It is a financial account tied to business activity, tax reporting, identity verification, and compliance rules. You do not need to be an LLC in every case. Sole proprietors and freelancers can be real businesses too.

Different countries have different rules. Different business structures exist. Sole proprietors exist. DBAs exist. LLCs exist. Some people may have legal ways to protect their privacy. Because once you start mixing personal spending, fake business details, tax information, payment processor rules, and privacy concerns together, you are no longer doing a cute internet hack. You are creating a financial mess. Protect your privacy, yes. But do it properly. Do not gamble with your payment account, your money, your tax situation, or your ability to take commissions because someone made a cheerful thread sound easy.
